etymology.description: Depicts a container -> borrowed phonetically for the plant name
etymology.interpretation: The character 豆(dòu, 'drum') originally depicted a type of container. It was later borrowed for its phonetic value to represent the plant name 'bean', distinguishing it from a container. This transition indicates how the physical shape of the container, resembling a drum or storage pot, facilitated a phonetic borrowing.

examples.chinese: 我们在课堂上学习如何制作豆(我們在課堂上學習如何製作豆)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài kètáng shàng xuéxí rúhé zhìzuò dòu.
examples.english: We are learning how to make drums in class.

examples.chinese: 村庄里有一个古老的豆(村莊裡有一個古老的豆)，它的声音很有魅力(它的聲音很有魅力)。
examples.pinyin: Cūnzhuāng lǐ yǒu yí gè gǔlǎo de dòu, tā de shēngyīn hěn yǒu mèilì.
examples.english: There is an ancient drum in the village, and its sound is captivating.

examples.chinese: 音乐会以豆声开场(音樂會以豆聲開場)，让观众立刻沉浸在气氛中(讓觀眾立刻沈浸在氣氛中)。
examples.pinyin: Yīnyuèhuì yǐ dòu shēng kāichǎng, ràng guānzhòng lìkè chénjìn zài qìfēn zhōng.
examples.english: The concert opened with the sound of drums, immersing the audience in the atmosphere immediately.
